articles.description:
Taco Bell adds new Chocolate Fudge & Caramel Empanadas to the menu as a limited-time dessert offering inspired by the discontinued Caramel Apple Empanada.

The mini empanadas are sold in pairs and priced at $2.99. One is filled with salted caramel and has a regular crust, while the other features a chocolate fudge filling and a chocolaty shell.
Additionally, the new, limited-time Sweet Empanadas & Cream Chiller joins the menu exclusively at Live Más Café locations across Southern California, Texas, and Las Vegas. The frozen drink features a vanilla ice cream base with swirls of caramel and Mexican chocolate syrup, finished with whipped cream.
Photo via Taco Bell.

articles.description:
Pizza Hut is running a limited-time "$10 Any Pizza" deal, where you can get a large or medium pizza on either Original Pan, Hand Tossed, Thin 'n Crispy, or Chicago Tavern-Style crust with up to five toppings for $10 at participating locations nationwide.

The $10 Any Pizza deal is only available for carryout. Stuffed crust and extra toppings are available for an additional charge (it's $3 more for stuffed crust at my local Pizza Hut).
A similar deal is running at rival Domino's.
Photo via Pizza Hut.

articles.title: Pizza Hut Offers "$10 Any Pizza" Deal (up to 5 toppings)

articles.description:
Wendy's serves up the new Cheesy Bacon Cheeseburger as its latest limited-time burger. The new menu item is joined by the new Chicken Tenders Ranch Wrap and the return of the chain's Crispy Panko Fish Sandwich.

The Cheesy Bacon Cheeseburger features a quarter-pound beef patty, applewood-smoked bacon, two slices of American cheese, and cheddar cheese sauce (the same sauce that you can get on the chain's baked potatoes), served on a toasted bun.
The Chicken Tenders Ranch Wrap is similar to the Grilled Chicken Wrap, only with chicken tenders instead of grilled chicken. It features two crispy-fried chicken tenders, ranch, lettuce, and shredded cheddar cheese, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla.
Available through April 5, 2026, Wendy's Crispy Panko Fish Sandwich features a crispy-fried, panko-crusted, wild-caught Alaskan pollock fish filet served with shredded lettuce, dill tartar sauce, American cheese, and crinkle-cut pickles on a toasted bun.
Prices vary, but the Cheesy Bacon Cheeseburger ranges from $7.29 (with a single patty) to $9.29 (with triple patties) at my local Wendy's here in Los Angeles. The Chicken Tenders Ranch Wrap goes for $6.79, while the Crispy Panko Fish Sandwich is $6.29.
Photo via Wendy's.

articles.description:
Taco Bell launches new Chicken Bacon Ranch Street Chalupas and Chicken Bacon Ranch Nacho Fries for a limited time.

Chicken Bacon Ranch Chalupas come two to an order and feature two toasted cheddar mini Chalupa shells filled with slow-roasted chicken (i.e., Cantina chicken), bacon, pico de gallo, shredded cheddar cheese, and Avocado Ranch Sauce. The suggested price per order is $5.99.
You can also get Chicken Bacon Ranch Street Chalupas as part of a $9 Discovery Luxe Box, which includes an order of Chicken Bacon Ranch Street Chalupas, a Cheesy Bean & Rice Burrito, chips and nacho cheese sauce, and a medium fountain drink.
Chicken Bacon Ranch Nacho Fries feature seasoned fries topped with slow-roasted chicken, bacon, warm nacho cheese sauce, Avocado Ranch Sauce, and pico de gallo. It carries a suggested price of $4.99.
Chicken Bacon Ranch is a combination that Taco Bell occasionally trots out on a limited-time basis. Other past items featuring the combination (that you probably don't remember) include the Bacon Ranch Tortada, Bacon Ranch Chicken Flatbread Sandwich, and more. The Bacon Club Chalupa was probably its most popular iteration.
Photo via Taco Bell.

articles.description:
McDonald's Big Arch is a limited-time burger that features two quarter-pound beef patties, three slices of pasteurized process white cheddar cheese, crispy-fried onions, slivered fresh onions, pickles, shredded iceberg lettuce, and new Big Arch Sauce, served on a toasted sesame and poppy seed bun.

I paid $8.59 for a Big Arch at my local McDonald's.



Big Arch Sauce is described by McDonald's as "the perfect balance of mustard, pickle, and sweet tomato flavors. It comes off as a more mustard-forward, more complex, tangier take on Big Mac Sauce. It's familiar but different. They forgot the pickles on my burger, but I didn't miss them, as the Big Arch Sauce provided plenty of acidity.

The bun was softer but less plush than the typical McDonald's sesame seed bun. It didn't have the same spring as the regular bun. The poppy seeds added some visual appeal and a smattering of texture.



The beef patties were moist, sporting a coarse grind and a well-seared crust, providing an enjoyable mix of flavor and texture. The white cheddar was milder than American cheese. It was nicely melted and complemented the beef well.
The crispy onions provided a bit of sweetness and delicate crispiness, while the slivered onions were good for a watery crunch and onion-y bite. The lettuce was mostly there for visual appeal and color.



Overall, McDonald's Big Arch turned out pretty well. It's basically a meatier, cheesier, and slightly fancier take on the chain's signature Big Mac (minus the middle bun).
Nutritional Info - McDonald's Big Arch
Calories - 1020
Fat - 65g (Saturated Fat - 25g)
Sodium - 1760mg
Carbs - 59g (Sugar - 10g)
Protein - 53g
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: McDonald's - Big Arch Burger

The deal can be used on boneless, traditional (bone-in) wings, or mix-and-match wings for carryout or dine-in (i.e., it's not available for delivery. You can pick up to three flavors, but dips will cost extra.
Prices vary, but an order of 15 traditional wings at Wingstop near me in Los Angeles typically costs $19.79 ($17.69 for boneless), which comes to about $1.31 per wing.
Photo via Wingstop.

articles.title: Wingstop Offers 30 Wings for $30

articles.description:
Trader Joe's Toasted Coconut Mini Sheet Cake is described on its lid as a "moist, flavorful toasted coconut cake with a toasted coconut cream cheese frosting." It's a limited-time, seasonal item.

An 18-oz cake cost me $5.99.



The Toasted Coconut Mini Sheet Cake was dense and very moist (from a combination of coconut milk and buttermilk). The combination of cake and frosting was sweet but not too sweet. It was less sweet than similar Trader Joe's cakes that I've tried.



There was a moderate and pleasant coconut flavor throughout, and the added texture of shredded coconut in both the icing and the cake. However, despite the description, I didn't pick up on any toasted coconut notes; it just tasted like plain coconut.



When it comes down to it, Trader Joe's Toasted Coconut Mini Sheet Cake made for good eating. I particularly enjoyed the flavor and sweetness level.
Nutritional Info - Trader Joe's Toasted Coconut Mini Sheet Cake
Serving Size - 1/6 cake (85g)
Calories - 320
Fat - 16g (Saturated Fat - 9g)
Sodium - 230mg
Carbs - 40g (Sugar - 28g)
Protein - 3g
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Trader Joe's - Toasted Coconut Mini Sheet Cake

With St. Patrick's Day on the horizon, Dairy Queen trots out the mint and launches the new Mint Crunchin' Cookie Dipped Cone while featuring the regular menu Mint Oreo Blizzard.

The Mint Crunchin' Cookie Dipped Cone features vanilla soft serve dipped in a green mint-flavored cone coating with chocolaty cookie crumbles.
Additionally, Dairy Queen offers a series of "Mint Crunchin' Cookie Hacks" that are available while the mint-flavored cone coating is around and include:


Mint Crunchin' Cookie Blizzard Treat & Sidecar - Vanilla soft serve mixed with Mint Crunchin' Cookie Cone Dip, served with a cup ("sidecar") of Mint Crunchin' Cookie Cone Dip so you can add some to every bite of the Blizzard.
Mint Crunchin' Cookie Parfait - Vanilla soft serve layered with Mint Crunchin' Cookie Cone Dip and Oreo Cookie pieces.


The Mint Oreo Blizzard is the Blizzard of the Month for March 2026 and features vanilla soft serve mixed with Oreo cookie pieces and creme de menthe.
While the Mint Oreo Blizzard is available year-round, the Mint' Crunchin' Cookie Dipped Cone is now available for a limited time at participating locations nationwide.
Photo via Dairy Queen.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Dairy Queen Debuts New Mint Crunchin' Cookie Dipped Cone

The cookie bakery chain features a limited-time weekly menu that usually includes four desserts (alongside a permanent "Classics" menu). This week's rotating lineup includes:
- New Strawberry Biscoff Cookie (pictured) - A warm cookie with a melty Biscoff center and crunchy Biscoff topping, finished with strawberry cream cheese frosting and a fresh strawberry slice.
- Cinnamon Square - A warm cinnamon cake square marbled with brown sugar-cinnamon butter and topped with a light vanilla cream cheese frosting.
- Sea Salt Toffee Cookie - A warm cookie packed with toffee and milk chocolate chips, topped with a sprinkle of flaky sea salt.
- Galaxy Brownie Cookie - A warm brownie cookie smothered in a warm fudge glaze and finished with a sprinkle of rainbow candy bits.
Photo via Crumbl Cookies.

articles.description:
Habit Burger & Grill launches its new Baja Crispy Fish sandwich alongside Cheesy Street Corn Tots and the Blue Wave Shake.

The Baja Crispy Fish sandwich features beer-battered wild Alaska cod served with cilantro-lime coleslaw, pickled red onions, and Baja sauce on a toasted brioche bun.
Cheesy Street Corn Tots are fried cheesy tots made with sweet corn, peppers, and fresh cilantro. A choice of dipping sauce is included (Avocado Ranch is the recommended pairing). 
The Blue Wave Shake features a swirled blue, vanilla shake mixed with pineapple and coconut flavors, topped with whipped cream.
Prices vary, but the Baja Crispy Fish costs $11.19 near me in Los Angeles, while Cheesy Street Corn Tots cost $4.99, and the Blue Wave Shake is $5.89.
All three items are available now for a limited time at participating locations chain-wide.
Photo via Habit Burger & Grill.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Habit Burger Introduces New Baja Crispy Fish Sandwich and More

articles.description:
McDonald's will be celebrating "National Egg McMuffin Day" on Monday, March 2, 2026, by offering a deal where you can get either an Egg McMuffin or a Sausage McMuffin with Egg for $1 exclusively through the McDonald's mobile app.

The Egg McMuffin features a fresh-cracked cage-free egg served with Canadian bacon and American cheese on a toasted and buttered English muffin.
The Sausage McMuffin with Egg is the same as an Egg McMuffin, only with a sausage patty instead of Canadian bacon.
Prices vary, but both breakfast sandwiches normally cost $4.99 at my local McDonald's here in Los Angeles.
The deal is limited to one discounted breakfast sandwich per customer during breakfast hours.
Photo via McDonald's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: $1 Egg McMuffin or Sausage McMuffin with Egg at McDonald's on March 2, 2026

articles.description:
Krispy Kreme partners with Oreo to feature two new, limited-time donuts and one regular-menu donut in the Krispy Kreme x Oreo Cookie Collection.

Available now at participating Krispy Kreme locations across the country, the collection includes:
- New Golden Oreo Donut - An Original Glazed donut topped with Golden Oreo-inspired buttercreme, Golden Oreo cookie crumbles, rainbow sprinkles, and a Mini Golden Oreo cookie.
- New Chocolate Oreo Dream Donut - An Original Glazed donut dipped in chocolate icing and Oreo cookie pieces, topped with chocolate-flavored Kreme and an Oreo wafer cookie.
- Oreo Cookies & Kreme Donut - A regular menu donut featuring a shell donut filled with Cookies & Kreme filling, dipped in chocolate icing, and topped with Oreo cookie pieces and a drizzle of white icing.
The donut chain is offering the Krispy Kreme x Oreo Cookie Dozen, which includes three of each of the donuts, plus three Original Glazed donuts. They're also available for purchase individually and in a special three-pack that includes one of each Oreo donut.
Photo via Krispy Kreme.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Krispy Kreme Welcomes New Golden Oreo Donut and More

articles.description:
Dutch Bros aims to evoke childhood nostalgia with the launch of a trio of new childhood-inspired beverages.

Available now for a limited time at all Dutch Bros locations, the lineup includes:
- Brown Butter Chocolate Chip - A drink swirled with a drizzle of brown butter chocolate chip sauce and topped with brown butter chocolate chip Soft Top (sweet cold foam) with chocolate crunch pieces. Dutch Bros recommends getting it as a latte, a protein latte, a Freeze, or a cold brew.
- Fruit Punch with Sour Candy Straw - A drink mixed with orange, cherry, and pineapple flavors, served with a sour candy straw. It is recommended by the chain as an iced or blended Rebel energy drink or a lemonade.
- Kool Blue - A beverage with watermelon and blue raspberry flavors, topped with mango poppin' boba. Give this flavor a try as an iced or blended Rebel energy drink or a lemonade!
Photo via Dutch Bros Coffee.
Read more at Brand Eating!
